mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Excessively low inflation (CPIAUCSL < 1.5%) signals weak demand and potential central bank intervention, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as markets anticipate rate cuts.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL < 1.5%, or inflation surprises exceed 2.5% in 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) and GDP (GDPC1) indicate strong supply-side capacity utilization, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through improved corporate earnings prospects.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.05 for any 3-year window, or 60-day returns in high INDPRO/GDPC1 growth periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A tightening labor market (UNRATE < 4.0%) signals wage pressures and potential inflation, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as investors price in monetary policy hawkishness.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 50% of instances where UNRATE < 4.0%, or wage growth surprises exceed 0.5% in 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST > 1200k) and consumer spending (RSAFS > 480k) indicate robust demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through improved corporate revenue outlooks.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.03 for any 2-year window, or 20-day returns in high HOUST/RSAFS periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS > 150k) signals a resilient labor market, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as corporate earnings prospects improve.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 50% of instances where PAYEMS > 150k, or job growth surprises exceed 20k in 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in consumer confidence (CPIAUCSL > 2.5% and HOUST < 1100k) signals weakening demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as recession risks rise.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL > 2.5% and HOUST < 1100k, or inflation surprises exceed 1.0% in 60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO > 105) and falling unemployment (UNRATE < 4.0%) indicate overheating supply-side capacity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as inflationary pressures force rate hikes.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where INDPRO > 105 and UNRATE < 4.0%, or industrial output surprises exceed 2.0% in 60% of cases.
